title: keckiella antirrhinoides var. antirrhinoides
intro: Keckiella antirrhinoides var. antirrhinoides, also known as the mountain monkey flower, is a perennial plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The mountain monkey flower can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, and its leaves and flowers are often harvested for their medicinal properties.
